reasoning: The NFCI Credit Subindex has exhibited strong, smooth downward momentum over the last 7-8 weeks, dropping from +0.022 on April 18 to -0.028 on June 6. This indicates steadily loosening credit conditions. Because the series exhibits high autocorrelation and smooth trends, the probability of a sudden reversal within a single week (by June 15) to a value higher than -0.028 is low. As the forecast horizon extends to July and September, the likelihood of a cycle bottom and subsequent tightening (rising index) increases, although the index often takes several months to complete a trough-to-peak cycle. By December, macro uncertainty dominates, making a return to the long-term trend or a market event much more plausible, pushing the probabilities closer to a coin toss.
